items.title: Algae which caused brevetoxins in SA algal bloom most toxic of its kind studied

children.content: The dominant species in the algal bloom which devastated SA's coastline,
Karenia cristata
, is the most toxic of its kind studied, according to newly published research.

gallery.altText: Dead marine life at Adelaide's West Beach.

thumbnail.altText: Dead marine life at Adelaide's West Beach.

avatar.alt: A headshot of a smiling woman with a dark background

items.title: Teen SA country footballer forced to find new sport after eight concussions

children.content: Angus Pitman finds a new sporting passion after walking away from country footy due to coping eight concussions on the field.

image.altText: a young man holds a red AFL football staring at camera

items.title: Suspected bird flu case detected in SA but yet to be confirmed as H5

children.content: A suspected H5 bird flu case has been detected in a giant petrel on SA's Yorke Peninsula, but the state government says the viral load is low, with further results expected within the next 48 hours.

image.altText: Two hands holding two canisters with yellow lids and red liquid

items.title: 'Faeces, urine and dirt': Piggery owners in court on animal cruelty charges

children.content: Five co-owners of an Adelaide Plains piggery are collectively accused of ill-treating their animals, including allegedly failing to ensure the animals had "adequate and appropriate living conditions".

image.altText: A man has short grey hair, he wears a dark jumper and walks outside a brick court building surrounded by reporters

items.title: SA premier condemns 'fight club' but teens vow to continue bouts

children.content: South Australia's premier says he is appalled by an amateur children's "fight club", while the state's police minister says people who post videos of the fights online may face legal action on top of the participants in the bouts.

image.altText: Two boys fighting while another two people watch on

items.title: Outback becomes wildlife oasis after 'unbelievable' desert rains

children.content: Winged explorers of all colours, shapes and sizes flock to the desert's edge to breed in the bustling wetlands of the Channel Country.

gallery.altText: A tiny golden bird nestled among dry grass in what looks to be fairly dry country.

thumbnail.altText: A tiny golden bird nestled among dry grass in what looks to be fairly dry country.

items.title: AFL legend Tony Modra 'overwhelmed by the kindness' after truck crash

children.content: Former Adelaide Crows and Fremantle player Tony Modra says he has left hospital after he was seriously injured in a truck crash last month.

image.altText: A man wearing a black shirt smiles.

items.title: Fishers buoyed as algal bloom-related whiting ban lifted early

children.content: A temporary ban on recreational catches of King George whiting along parts of the SA coast is lifted earlier than scheduled, in a move that fishers say will benefit the whole sector.

image.altText: Two King George whiting in hands of a man.

items.title: Man denied bail after drugs allegedly seized from vehicle in prison car park

children.content: Wayne Douglas Carnell has been remanded in custody after police allegedly found 100 grams of methamphetamine in his vehicle which was searched in a prison car park.

image.altText: Bags of white powder and colourful containers taken from a bird's eye view.

items.title: The 'walking archive' helping First Nations people discover family history

children.content: For 28 years, Ali Abdullah-Highfold has helped thousands of Indigenous people find familial connections through his work at the South Australian Museum.

image.altText: A man looks at the camera while books are on a table in front of him

items.title: Farmer celebrates lamb birth boom as twins and triplets thrive

children.content: A wet start to 2026 in regional South Australia has helped create perfect conditions for multiple lamb births on one merino sheep stud.

image.altText: A man with a red hat is smiling holding two black and white speckled sheep.

items.title: SA childcare centre closes for three months after 'very serious' allergy incident

children.content: South Australia's Education Standards Board says it has "serious concerns" about an Edge Early Learning centre after a child was taken to hospital following an allergen incident, with the centre directed to
close immediately for the second time this year.

image.altText: A childcare centre on a quiet street

items.title: The faces in the crowd at the Ayatollah's funeral

children.content: The strictly choreographed funeral of Iran's former supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has presented a rare insight into the image Tehran wants to project to the world and the men they see as central to it.

gallery.altText: Iran's leadership team appeared at Ali Khamenei's funeral.

thumbnail.altText: Iran's leadership team appeared at Ali Khamenei's funeral.

avatar.alt: A close up photo of a brunette woman wearing glasses.

items.title: Far-right UK political leader quits as MP, vows to recontest seat

children.content: Nigel Farage, the far-right political leader riding a surge of support in Britain, has announced a major gamble that could end his political career.

image.altText: A man in a suit looks on with a disappointed look on his face.

items.title: Parents of child abused by Sydney care worker want ban on male educators

children.content: The devastated parents of a child abused by disgraced childcare worker David James are calling for urgent reform to the out-of-school-hours care system to better protect children.

image.altText: A CCTV still of David James buying a drink at the Orchard Hotel.

images.altText: Lionel Messi is thrown in the air by the rest of the Argentina squad
Switzerland players celebrate after winning a penalty shootout

articles.title: 'Perversion of justice': Politicians launch bid to investigate FIFA boss

children.content: A group of European politicians launches a bid to begin an investigation into how and why a red card for an American football star was overturned at the World Cup.

image.altText: Donald Trump and Gianni Infantino in blue suits peering over the golden FIFA Club World Cup trophy on the Oval Office desk

articles.title: 'We have suffered injustice': Egypt coach fumes at ref after Argentina loss

children.content: Egypt coach Hossam Hassan says his side "suffered an injustice" in defeat to Argentina, raging at the referee for a number of perceived errors.

image.altText: Hossam Hassan yells in anger on the touchline

children.content: Lionel Messi guides Argentina to an incredible comeback win against Egypt, coming back from 2-0 down to steal a 3-2 win at the death, while Switzerland knocks Colombia out in a penalty shootout. Follow live.

items.title: Messi leads incredible Argentina comeback before Swiss shootout delight
PM prepares to host leaders of three Pacific nations in Brisbane

children.content: The prime minister will host the leaders of PNG, Tonga and Samoa in Brisbane today for regional security talks after signing of the Ocean of Peace Alliance with Fiji. Follow live.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese at Pacific Islands Forum

items.title: China missile test hits a bullseye in the Pacific — but it's one they didn't want

children.content: This week, perhaps for the first time in a long time, some sections of the Pacific are signalling a slight adjustment to their relationship with China.

image.altText: A man in a suit in a hospital environment with young girls nearby and a solomon islands flag

items.title: Netanyahu warns Trump against F-35 deal with Türkiye

children.content: The Israeli prime minister has appeared on American TV to urge the US president not to sell jet fighters to Türkiye, adding to a growing list of tensions between the leaders.

articles.title: Huge arms deals struck at NATO but Trump 'disappointed' with leaders

children.content: In a sign they are heeding calls from the United States to spend more to defend Europe, NATO leaders agree to deals worth about $72 billion.

image.altText: Donald Trump walks alongside dozens of men in military uniform holding guns.

gallery.altText: Donald Trump and Minister Benjamin Netanyahu stand shoulder to shoulder and pose for a photo at the entrance of the White House.

thumbnail.altText: Donald Trump and Minister Benjamin Netanyahu stand shoulder to shoulder and pose for a photo at the entrance of the White House.

items.title: 'Teeth can heal': Oral health giant's ad claims called into question

children.content: Oral care giant Hismile has used "misleading" videos in social media advertisements showing a sales professional, and its own staff, spruiking products while dressed as health workers.

image.altText: A composite of two scenes from a Hismile ad

items.title: Marine Le Pen to enter French presidential race as court trims ban

children.content: The court in Paris upheld Marine Le Pen's conviction for misusing EU funds but shortened her ban on running for elected office.

image.altText: A 60-year-old woman in a pink business suit walks down courtroom corridor

items.title: Five podcasts we're listening to this NAIDOC Week

children.content: Some of our favourite Indigenous-led podcasts this week and every week include interviews with AFL greats, a dive into Aboriginal astronomy, and conversations with a renowned artist who loves Dolly Parton.

articles.title: 'Oldest continually practised medicine' being passed on to students

children.content: Aboriginal Australians have used native plants as bush medicine for tens of thousands of years. On Larrakia land, medical students are learning how nature can heal.

image.altText: A man wearing a brown hat takes a bite of a small passionfruit.

items.title: Quiz: Jump in, we're going on a trip around the world

children.content: Which Mediterranean island was a setting for The Godfather? And what's the capital city of Morocco?

image.altText: Aerial of Capetown

items.title: What time is the State of Origin Game III decider tonight?

children.content: The men's State of Origin series will be decided tonight, as Queensland hosts New South Wales at Lang Park in a winner-takes-all Game III. Here is what you need to know ahead of kick-off.

image.altText: Mark Nawaqanitawase diving for the try line, extending the football with one hand, with three defenders trying to push him out

items.title: Prince Harry loses privacy case against Daily Mail, calls ruling 'whitewash'

children.content: Prince Harry and six other claimants, including singer Elton John, have lost their claims against Daily Mail's publisher after alleging their phones were tapped, voicemails intercepted and personal information obtained through deception.

items.title: Government report says AI not causing mass job lay-offs, yet

children.content: Artificial intelligence is not yet causing a broad disruption to Australia's labour market, according to a government report.

image.altText: The hand of a robot and human touch a screen

items.title: Toddler waits seven hours for stitches during patient surge

children.content: A Central Victorian mother says she waited seven hours at Bendigo Health for her toddler to be treated for a head wound.

image.altText: A little girl sitting in an ambulance bed with a head wound.

items.title: Teammates of Melbourne footballer will sit next match out after death

children.content: Epping football club's reserves players have decided to skip this weekend's game following the death of their beloved teammate Nathan Fitzgerald on Monday night.

items.title: Housing inequity in Australia 'must focus beyond age'

children.content: The federal government must look at age and wealth together to meaningfully deal with the growing wealth divide between generations, according to The Australian Council of Social Services (ACOSS).

image.altText: an outside view of a row of houses

items.title: Djokovic wins five-hour Wimbledon epic to set up Sinner blockbuster

children.content: Tennis legend Novak Djokovic sets up a Wimbledon semifinal blockbuster against Jannik Sinner after a five-hour epic.

image.altText: GettyImages-2284558415

items.title: 'I'm getting old': 28yo Osaka's Wimbledon run ends at quarterfinals

children.content: Japan's Naomi Osaka bemoans getting old as her inspiring Wimbledon run comes to an end in the quarterfinals.

image.altText: A tennis player grimaces at the sky while standing in front of a clock.

items.title: Russia could compete at 2028 Olympics after suspension provisionally lifted

children.content: The International Olympic Committee has not yet decided whether Russia can display its flag and colours or have its anthem played at the Games.

image.altText: The Olympic and Russian flags fly together

items.title: Locals fundraising to stop beachside town slipping into the ocean

children.content: Lancelin residents have been left to fundraise $150,000 for a sea wall in a bid to fight coastal erosion now seriously threatening the town, more than 100 kilometres north of Perth.

image.altText: Aerial of a building sitting near a sand cliff and the ocean.

items.title: Seventeen dead, 900 snakes set loose as storms lash China

children.content: China's president calls for "all out" rescue efforts after storms kill at least 17 people and force the evacuation of tens of thousands.

image.altText: An aerial photo of a houses in flood water.

items.title: Explosions rock Damascus near hotel hosting French president Macron

children.content: French officials say Emmanuel Macron did not hear the blasts, and his motorcade continued through the streets of the Syrian capital to a meeting with Syrian President Ahmed Al-Sharaa, in the first visit by a European head of state since 2024.
